summary.intent: Address a batch of Codex automated review findings on PR #724 in fogodev/ars-ui, covering localized form submit status messages, unconditional field aria-describedby synthesis, Dioxus spec type drift for errors, and missing controlled errors prop on Fieldset components.
summary.outcome: All five Codex review threads were addressed and resolved: core Fieldset gained a controlled errors prop mirroring Field, form submit failure messages were localized via ars_forms::form::Messages, field input aria-describedby was tightened to only reference actually-mounted descriptions, Dioxus Fieldset description registration was updated to use stable signal guards, and the Dioxus Field spec was corrected to Vec<Error>. The batch was committed and pushed, with all format, clippy, wasm compile, and focused SSR tests passing.

repo: Fieldset previously lacked a controlled errors prop that Field already had; the pattern for controlled error data needed to be mirrored from ars-components Field to Fieldset, then wired through both Leptos and Dioxus adapters.

Form submit failure status messages must be localized through ars_forms::form::Messages rather than using hardcoded English strings; the core form machine uses Messages = () so the adapter submit handler resolves them.

The raw Field and Fieldset parts intentionally cannot guarantee SSR description-ID relationships when child-order composition is arbitrary — this is a documented low-level building-block tradeoff, not a bug to fix at the raw-parts layer.
Dioxus Fieldset description registration should use the same stable signal guard + on_drop unregistration pattern used by Dioxus Field to ensure SSR markup correctness.
Dioxus provider tests were using a stale use_messages API that no longer exists; the correct grouped API is use_messages_and_locale, matching the Leptos side.
The Leptos/Dioxus adapter tests for fieldset and form SSR are in crates/ars-leptos/tests/fieldset.rs, crates/ars-dioxus/tests/fieldset.rs, and corresponding form.rs files.

Explicit fieldset::Props struct literals in both crates/ars-components/src/utility/fieldset/mod.rs and crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/utility/mod.rs needed the new errors field added when the core prop was added.
The CI suite uses cargo xtask ci fmt, cargo xclippy, and separate wasm target checks (cargo +nightly check --tests --target wasm32-unknown-unknown) as distinct gates.

code.finding: Added controlled errors prop to core Fieldset Props, mirroring the existing pattern in Field. Two explicit Props struct literals in this file and in the proptest file needed updating.
Form submit failure status text must be resolved through ars_forms::form::Messages for localization; was previously using hardcoded English.
Same localized form submit message fix as Leptos adapter.
Field input aria-describedby was unconditionally synthesizing a description ID; fixed to only emit the relationship when the machine's actual has_description/error state is true.
Same aria-describedby conditional fix as Leptos Field adapter.
Fieldset description registration updated to use stable signal guard + on_drop unregistration pattern, matching Field's approach for SSR correctness.
Provider tests were using stale use_messages API; updated to use_messages_and_locale grouped API to match current implementation and fix wasm compile.
Dioxus Field spec had type drift — errors prop documented as wrong type; corrected to Vec<Error>.
Updated to document new controlled errors prop.
Updated to document new controlled errors prop.

workflow: The Codex review loop polls PR reactions (👀 = in progress, 👍 = approved, no reaction after 👀 drops = findings posted) and unresolved thread count to determine review state.
Codex review threads are resolved via GitHub GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation; replies are posted via gh api -X POST repos/.../pulls/.../comments with in_reply_to.
New Codex review passes are triggered by posting '@codex review' as a PR comment via gh pr comment.
When adding a new field to a core Props struct in Rust, all explicit struct literal instantiations across tests must be found and updated — grep for 'Props {' in the affected crate and tests.

SSR assertion strings should be loosened to require the presence of a specific ID within aria-describedby rather than requiring the exact full attribute value, to avoid brittleness when multiple IDs are legitimately present.
Run cargo +nightly fmt --all before submitting to CI to avoid format-only failures; cargo xtask ci fmt validates the result.
Focused test runs per adapter+test file (cargo test -p ars-leptos --test fieldset --features ssr) are faster than full suite runs for verifying specific fixes.

friction: Long commit hook execution required repeated write_stdin waits before the push could proceed.
Codex review took many polling cycles (>10 minutes of 120s sleeps) before producing findings, requiring sustained session patience.

The Leptos Fieldset SSR assertion initially failed because the fixed implementation correctly emitted both description and error IDs, but the test expected only the error ID — the test needed to be loosened rather than the code being wrong.
Dioxus wasm compile check revealed stale provider test API usage (use_messages vs use_messages_and_locale) unrelated to the review batch, which had to be fixed as a prerequisite.
An unused test variable in Dioxus tests generated a compiler warning that needed cleanup before clippy would pass cleanly.
open_items: The raw Field and Fieldset parts still cannot guarantee SSR description-ID relationships when children are composed in arbitrary order — the proper fix requires a higher-level TextField component that owns the composition and knows all child slots upfront. This is an approved low-level building-block tradeoff deferred to the TextField follow-up.

The Dioxus Fieldset description SSR relationship for arbitrary child-order composition was improved with stable signal guards but the fundamental SSR constraint (root attrs emitted before children render) remains a known limitation for raw-part composition.
CI coverage job was still pending at last check during the review wait — outcome not confirmed in this session.
